title: mangifera mekongensis
intro: Mangifera mekongensis, also known as the Southeast Asian mango, is a species of mango tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which provide various health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Mangifera mekongensis are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
